title: How much you can get - Canada.ca
content:
## How much you can get
Status: Will be replaced in July 2026
The GST/HST credit payments are not taxable and credit amounts will vary based on your situation. You do not have to report the GST/HST payments you receive on your tax return.

## How your GST/HST credit is calculated
Your GST/HST credit payments are based on the following:
- Your adjusted family net income
- The number of eligible children under 19 years old that you have registered for the Canada child benefit, GST/HST credit, or both
For details: GST/HST credit calculation sheets

### Payment amounts are recalculated every July
Your GST/HST credit amount is recalculated every July based on the information from your previous year's tax return. Each GST/HST credit payment period starts in July and ends in June of the following year.
For example, the information from your 2024 tax return determines the GST/HST credit amount you get for the payment period from July 2025 to June 2026. You could get up to:
- $533 if you are a single individual
- $698 if you are married or have a common-law partner
- $184 for each child under the age of 19
For details: GST/HST credit payment amounts
An increase of your 2024 adjusted family net income could affect your payments starting in July 2025.
Find out the GST/HST credit amount you may be entitled to: GST/HST credit payments chart

### Additional one-time GST/HST credit top-up payment
The new Canada Groceries and Essentials Benefit (CGEB) will replace the GST/HST credit in July 2026. As part of the transition to the CGEB, an additional one-time top-up payment will be issued starting on June 5, 2026, to provide immediate support to individuals who were entitled to receive the GST/HST credit in January 2026.
For details: One-time GST/HST credit top-up payment

### What affects your payment calculation
The Canada Revenue Agency recalculates your GST/HST credit payment when:
- A reassessment of either your or your spouse or common-law partner's tax return results in a change to your adjusted family net income
- Your eligible child turns 19 years of age
- Your marital status changes
- The number of eligible children in your care changes
- You start, or stop, sharing custody of a child
Be sure to keep your personal information up to date to avoid any disruption to your payments.

## Estimate your credit amount
You can use the child and family benefits calculator to find out how much your GST/HST credit payments may be, and which other benefits and credits you can get.
Child and family benefits calculator

## Where to find your credit amount
If you are entitled to the credit, the CRA will send you a GST/HST credit notice based on the information from your assessed tax return. The notice provides your annual entitlement along with the payment schedule for your quarterly payments.
If your adjusted family net income is too high, the CRA will not send you a notice since you are not entitled to receive the credit .
If you do not have a CRA account, the CRA will send your notice by mail.

### If you have a CRA account
You can sign in to your CRA account to view:
- Your next expected payment date and amount
- The status and amount of each payment for the year, from July to the following June
- Your statement of account for the GST/HST credit, including details about amounts and entitlements, dates, and balances
CRA sign in Register for a CRA account
- Choose your Individual account
- On the "Overview" screen, find your next expected payment under "Benefits and credits"
- Select Benefits and credits to find detailed payment information
- Go to GST/HST Credit to find your next expected payment date and amount, payment schedule, and your statement of account
If you have a spouse or common-law partner, your adjusted net incomes are combined to get your adjusted family net income (AFNI).

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
